other_observers: Sue Riffe and 10 others in a birding tour group; original finder uncertain; I believe Andrew Spencer independently reported this bird from either April 19 or April 20 to Cobirds.
specific_location: Below dam at Two Buttes State Wildlife Area
description: A large square-tailed dove, pale brown overall, with a white stripe on the wing visible in flight and to a lesser extent on the sitting bird; undertail blackish anteriorly and pale whitish posteriorly. Iris red, blue eye-ring and blue skin at base of bill, which was gray.Behaviors: Sitting high in a deciduous tree. Flew short distances within treetop.Call: Called several times, a seried of coos in a pattern reminiscent of the "who-cooks-for-you" call of the Barred OwlPlumage: Adult
